description:
Capture a fresh page-load latency baseline. Since the original ticket was filed:

- CPU cap was raised from 0.5 → 2.0 in docker-compose.yml (verify before measuring, since perf assumptions changed)
- Memory cap is now 2560M (raised 2026-04-23)
- Activity-page index/query work landed
- Killmails v2 disk-backed storage all 3 flags ON (2026-04-24)

Procedure:
1. Set VIGILANT_PERF_LOG=1, exercise the dashboard + slow routes (activity, killmails, fitting, star map, structure timers).
2. Use middleware timing — handler spans miss Jinja render time. Refer to `feedback_page_load_perf` memory for known traps: `now` name shadowing in templates, cache_stats SELECT scan, public ESI `db` flag default, token-refresh serialization.
3. Identify the top 3 slow routes and file each as its own issue (severity by user-visible impact).
4. Confirm killmails v2 stays within the 2 req/s ESI budget and that disk-first skip in fetch_killmail is firing (per `project_disk_backed_killmails`).

Measurement only — no code change in this ticket. Remediation issues are filed off the findings.
title: Re-baseline page-load perf with VIGILANT_PERF_LOG=1
